name: Daylighting Zone — Cortland @ Bocana
description: Remove parking at the corners of Cortland and Bocana to improve visibility for pedestrians crossing. Pursuant to CA Vehicle Code 22500(l) daylighting requirements. Proposed planters to mark the zone.

name: Asphalt Art Mural — Cortland @ Ellsworth
description: Community-designed mural in the intersection at Ellsworth to improve visibility and create a sense of place. Would feature Bernal Heights imagery and bright colors to alert drivers approaching the intersection.

name: Flex Posts — Mid-block Cortland
description: Flexible delineator posts installed mid-block on Cortland between Andover and Bocana to protect the parking-protected bike lane and discourage illegal parking that blocks sight lines.

name: Daylighting Zone — Cortland @ Andover
description: High-priority daylighting location at Andover. Significant pedestrian crossing activity and blind corners due to parked vehicles. Community has requested planters or paint markings.

name: Crosswalk Refresh — Cortland @ Folsom
description: Repaint faded crosswalk markings and add high-visibility ladder-style markings. Also proposing a pedestrian refuge island in the median to allow two-stage crossing.

name: Planter Daylighting — Cortland @ Gates
description: Decorative concrete planters at all four corners of the Cortland/Gates intersection as part of the neighborhood daylighting planter program. Community volunteers would maintain planting.

name: Speed Cushions — Cortland between Prospect & Bonview
description: Two speed cushions installed to reduce vehicle speeds on this residential block. Speed cushions (not full bumps) allow emergency vehicles to pass while still slowing regular traffic.

name: Asphalt Art — Cortland @ Coso
description: Bright asphalt art mural at the Coso intersection to increase driver awareness. Part of the Safe Streets Bernal beautification + safety program. Design to be community-selected.
